channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
item.description: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now features a new ecosystem of third-party integrations, allowing developers to connect their AI agents to various tools like GitHub and Notion. This enhancement aims to facilitate the creation of more capable real-world applications.

item.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
item.description: Google I/O 2026 will take place on May 19-20, featuring a save the date puzzle that showcases AI's role in enhancing event experiences. This annual tradition builds excitement ahead of the main event.

item.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
item.description: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas will focus on networking and hands-on problem solving, featuring technical tracks on agentic AI and security. The event emphasizes the benefits of in-person participation for developers.

item.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
item.description: Google has launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, which enhances GPU and NPU acceleration and supports PyTorch and JAX. This update aims to improve efficiency and security within the TensorFlow ecosystem.

item.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
item.description: Google Cloud has introduced Wednesday Build Hour, a weekly interactive session for developers to enhance their cloud skills through hands-on building. The program focuses on advanced topics like AI agents and productivity tools.

item.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
item.description: The Gemini Code Assist team has rolled out updates to streamline coding workflows, including features like Agent Mode and Inline Diff Views. These tools aim to transform AI into a more tailored coding collaborator.

item.title: Introducing Finish Changes and Outlines, now available in Gemini Code Assist extensions on IntelliJ and VS Code
item.description: Google has introduced new features for Gemini Code Assist in IntelliJ and VS Code, including Finish Changes and Outlines, which aim to reduce developer friction and improve code comprehension. These tools help streamline coding processes.

item.title: GitHub availability report: March 2026
item.description: The GitHub availability report for March 2026 details four incidents that led to degraded performance across its services. This report highlights ongoing reliability challenges.

item.title: Why I stopped using 'Modern Standby' on my Windows laptop to save battery overnight
item.description: An article discusses the drawbacks of using 'Modern Standby' on Windows laptops for overnight battery saving, suggesting that sleep mode may not be the best option. It offers insights into better battery preservation methods.

item.title: KDE Linux is the purest form of Plasma I've tested - but the install isn't for the meek
item.description: KDE Linux offers a pure version of the KDE Plasma desktop environment, but the installation process may be challenging for less experienced users. This distro is noted for its clean and customizable interface.

item.title: I found a hidden Google Photos tool that makes clearing storage feel less like a chore
item.description: Google Photos introduced a new feature for Android users that allows them to easily swipe to keep or delete photos, simplifying storage management.

item.title: Copilot-reviewed pull request merge metrics now in the usage metrics API
item.description: GitHub's Copilot usage metrics API now includes new metrics for pull request merges, building on previously released throughput and cycle-time metrics.

item.title: Meta's latest model is as open as Zuckerberg's private school
item.description: Meta's CEO Mark Zuckerberg has shifted from promoting open-source AI to embracing proprietary models, raising questions about the future of open AI.

item.title: Outlook: Upgraded Video and Audio Player for iOS
item.description: Outlook for iOS has upgraded its video and audio player, offering improved controls and smoother playback, set to launch in May 2026.

item.title: Microsoft Teams: AI-generated meeting archive for knowledge retention
item.description: Microsoft Teams is introducing an AI-generated meeting archive feature that allows retention of key insights beyond standard transcript periods.

item.title: Microsoft Teams: Entra passwordless resource account support for Teams Rooms on Windows devices
item.description: Teams Rooms on Windows will support passwordless sign-in through Entra Resource Accounts, enhancing security and user identity management.

item.title: Western Union zaps VMware and moves to Nutanix
item.description: Western Union is migrating from VMware to Nutanix, seeking to avoid business with Broadcom amid broader industry shifts.

item.title: Conflicting Rulings Leave Anthropic in ‘Supply-Chain Risk’ Limbo
item.description: Conflicting court rulings create uncertainty for Anthropic regarding the US military's use of its Claude AI model, posing supply-chain risks.

item.title: Atlassian gussies up Confluence for the AI era
item.description: Atlassian is updating Confluence with AI capabilities to help users convert notes into graphics and software applications, modernizing the platform.

item.title: Criminal wannabes even more dangerous than the pros, says ex-FBI cyber chief
item.description: A former FBI cyber chief warns that inexperienced criminals pose a significant threat, potentially leading to irreversible data loss.

item.title: You can read PDFs and articles on your Kindle: How to send all kinds of files to your device
item.description: Users can now easily send PDFs and articles to their Kindle devices, enhancing the reading experience with various file types. This feature allows for greater accessibility of content on Kindle.

item.title: Poke makes using AI agents as easy as sending a text
item.description: Poke simplifies the use of AI agents by allowing users to interact with them via text messages, eliminating the need for complex setups. This development makes AI more accessible to everyday users.

item.title: DARPA looking for battery that could power a laptop for months
item.description: DARPA is collaborating with a fusion energy startup to develop a radioactive battery capable of powering laptops for months. This innovation could revolutionize energy consumption for portable devices.

item.title: Iran-linked hackers disrupt operations at US critical infrastructure sites
item.description: Iran-linked hackers have intensified their cyberattacks on US critical infrastructure amid escalating tensions with Israel. This disruption poses serious risks to national security.

item.title: Call your existing automation ‘zero-token architecture’ to become an instant agentic AI wiz
item.description: Kelsey Hightower suggests that IT professionals can enhance productivity by rebranding existing automations as 'zero-token architecture.' This approach aims to adapt to the rise of agentic AI.

item.title: AWS boss explains why investing billions in both Anthropic and OpenAI is an OK conflict
item.description: AWS's CEO defends the company's investment in both Anthropic and OpenAI, arguing that competition is a natural part of their business model. This strategy reflects AWS's approach to partnerships.

item.title: Secret scanning improvements to alert APIs, webhooks, and delegated workflows
item.description: GitHub has announced improvements to its secret scanning features, enhancing APIs, webhooks, and delegated workflows. These updates aim to bolster the developer experience.

item.title: Customize Amazon Nova models with Amazon Bedrock fine-tuning
item.description: This guide details how to fine-tune Amazon Nova models using Amazon Bedrock, providing step-by-step instructions for optimizing model performance. It emphasizes the importance of quality training data.

item.title: Human-in-the-loop constructs for agentic workflows in healthcare and life sciences
item.description: AI agents are increasingly used in healthcare for tasks like data processing and regulatory compliance, but human oversight remains crucial. The article discusses implementing human-in-the-loop systems.

item.title: Building intelligent audio search with Amazon Nova Embeddings: A deep dive into semantic audio understanding
item.description: The article explores building intelligent audio search systems using Amazon Nova Embeddings, focusing on how audio can be represented as vectors for effective searching. It includes practical coding examples.

item.title: Reinforcement fine-tuning on Amazon Bedrock: Best practices
item.description: This article discusses reinforcement fine-tuning (RFT) on Amazon Bedrock, highlighting its effectiveness using the GSM8K dataset and offering best practices for dataset preparation and hyperparameter tuning.

item.title: This AirTag alternative with a 130dB siren and strobe light has my name written all over it
item.description: The Pebblebee Halo is a Bluetooth tracker that doubles as a personal safety device, featuring a loud siren and strobe light for emergencies.

item.title: GitHub Mobile: Research and code with Copilot cloud agent anywhere
item.description: GitHub Mobile now allows users to utilize the Copilot cloud agent for research and coding on-the-go, expanding its functionality beyond pull requests.

item.title: How to quickly convert EPUB files to Kindle format - and why it matters
item.description: This guide explains how to convert EPUB files to Kindle format, providing users with more control over their e-book collections.

item.title: Your Kindle's not obsolete, it just needs a jailbreak - and I'll show you how it's done
item.description: Learn how to jailbreak your Kindle to transform it into an open-source e-reader, even if the device is bricked.

item.title: Code Security risk assessment available for organizations
item.description: GitHub now provides a free Code Security risk assessment tool for organizations to identify and summarize security vulnerabilities.

item.title: GitHub Universe is back: We want you to take the stage
item.description: GitHub Universe is returning, inviting participants to share memorable sessions and experiences from past events.

item.title: How Amazon uses agentic AI for vulnerability detection at global scale
item.description: Amazon's RuleForge system utilizes agentic AI to create detection rules for vulnerabilities 336% faster than traditional methods, improving security measures.

item.title: New PGP signing key for GitHub CLI Linux packages
item.description: An updated PGP signing keyring for GitHub CLI's Linux packages has been released, ensuring secure package management.

item.title: Cloud Cost Optimization: How to maximize ROI from AI, manage costs, and unlock real business value
item.description: This article provides strategies for cloud cost optimization, focusing on maximizing ROI from AI investments while managing costs effectively.

item.title: ALTK‑Evolve: On‑the‑Job Learning for AI Agents
item.description: ALTK-Evolve introduces on-the-job learning for AI agents, enhancing their ability to adapt and improve in real-time environments. This development signifies a step forward in creating more autonomous and capable AI systems.

item.title: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio Code, March Releases
item.description: GitHub Copilot has released updates in Visual Studio Code, enhancing features like Autopilot. These improvements aim to streamline coding processes for developers.

item.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
item.description: During National Robotics Week, NVIDIA showcased advancements in physical AI and robotics, highlighting their impact on various industries. This reflects the growing integration of AI in real-world applications.

item.title: truthlocks-openai 1.0.0
item.description: Truthlocks has launched a MAIP wrapper for OpenAI that auto-generates cryptographic receipts for API calls, enhancing security and accountability in AI interactions.

item.title: truthlocks-openai added to PyPI
item.description: Truthlocks MAIP wrapper for OpenAI has been added to PyPI, allowing developers to easily implement cryptographic receipts for API calls. This addition supports better security practices.

item.title: truthlocks-langchain added to PyPI
item.description: Truthlocks has introduced a MAIP integration for LangChain, enabling automatic generation of cryptographic receipts for actions within chains and agents. This enhances security for developers using LangChain.

item.title: truthlocks-langchain 1.0.0
item.description: The Truthlocks MAIP integration for LangChain has been released, providing developers with tools to auto-generate cryptographic receipts for their actions. This aims to improve accountability in AI workflows.

item.title: How do I integrate OpenAI or Azure OpenAI API with a C# .NET 9 application
item.description: A guide has been published on integrating OpenAI and Azure OpenAI APIs with C# .NET 9 applications, detailing setup and secure API management. This resource aims to help developers leverage AI features effectively.

item.title: coding-proxy 0.1.4a3
item.description: The coding-proxy 0.1.4a3 has been released, offering a multi-vendor proxy for Claude Code, supporting various AI tools. This aims to enhance accessibility and functionality for developers.

item.title: mcp-synology 0.4.1
item.description: The MCP server for Synology NAS has been updated to version 0.4.1, allowing users to manage files via Claude. This integration enhances usability for NAS users.
